headline: AI use in UK hits 'tipping point' as companies scale up, Google exec says
summary: Adoption of artificial intelligence has reached a "tipping point" in Britain, as companies move from experimentation ‌to large-scale deployment and start seeing returns, a Google Cloud executive ‌said on Wednesday.  Businesses and government bodies that were testing AI tools a year ago are now ​using them to run more complex processes and improve productivity, Maureen Costello, Google Cloud's vice president for the United Kingdom, Ireland and Sub-Saharan Africa, told Reuters in an interview.

content.title: AI use in UK hits 'tipping point' as companies scale up, Google exec says
content.summary: Adoption of artificial intelligence has reached a "tipping point" in Britain, as companies move from experimentation ‌to large-scale deployment and start seeing returns, a Google Cloud executive ‌said on Wednesday.  Businesses and government bodies that were testing AI tools a year ago are now ​using them to run more complex processes and improve productivity, Maureen Costello, Google Cloud's vice president for the United Kingdom, Ireland and Sub-Saharan Africa, told Reuters in an interview.

headline: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to boost competition
summary: Britain's competition watchdog ordered Google to provide greater transparency on how its search rankings work, part ‌of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses and addressing ‌concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.  Google must rank organic search results ​using objective criteria, increase transparency around rankings, introduce clearer complaint processes, and allow users to transfer their search data to authorised third parties, the Competition and Markets Authority said on Wednesday.  A Google spokesperson said it was committed to protecting the integrity ‌of its systems and would ⁠work with the CMA, adding the company's ranking systems were "fair, transparent and show the most relevant, highest quality results".

content.title: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to boost competition
content.summary: Britain's competition watchdog ordered Google to provide greater transparency on how its search rankings work, part ‌of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses and addressing ‌concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.  Google must rank organic search results ​using objective criteria, increase transparency around rankings, introduce clearer complaint processes, and allow users to transfer their search data to authorised third parties, the Competition and Markets Authority said on Wednesday.  A Google spokesperson said it was committed to protecting the integrity ‌of its systems and would ⁠work with the CMA, adding the company's ranking systems were "fair, transparent and show the most relevant, highest quality results".

headline: Intel's 18A-P chips are in production and just in time as CPU demand ramps up
summary: Intel (INTC) announced that its 18A-P process node — a key component to the company's chips and foundry business — has entered initial production. Intel's stock is getting a slight lift in Wednesday trading. Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley dives into the specs of the new hardware and its significance to the AI chipmaker.

headline: Linux Foundation Launches Appia Foundation to Establish Standardized Conformity Specifications Across the AI Value Chain
summary: The Linux Foundation, the nonprofit organization enabling mass innovation through open source, today announced the formation of the Appia Foundation. Hosted under the Joint Development Foundation (JDF), the Appia Foundation will establish modular specifications that provide a connecting layer to bridge foundational global standards with practical, trusted assessments across the global AI value chain.

headline: Uber Robo-Taxis Move Step Closer as Self-Driving War Revs Up
summary: On Wednesday, ride-hailing giant  Uber  EV maker  Lucid  and self-driving technology start-up Nuro announced plans to launch robo-taxis in Houston in 2027.  Uber operates the network, Lucid builds the cars, and Nuro provides the autonomous driving technology.  The move is yet another example that the long-promised era of driverless cars has arrived.

content.title: Uber Robo-Taxis Move Step Closer as Self-Driving War Revs Up

Uber operates the network, Lucid builds the cars, and Nuro provides the autonomous driving technology.  The move is yet another example that the long-promised era of driverless cars has arrived.

headline: KLIPY raises $3.8M from investors and joins Google AI Futures Fund to build the expression layer for GIFs, memes, stickers, clips, and AI media
summary: KLIPY today announced a $3.8 million fundraising round, and that it has joined Google's AI Futures Fund, to scale its expression API layer for memes, stickers, clips, and AI for real-time expression. KLIPY helps developers integrate expressive media and AI APIs into social and creative platforms like BeReal, Baidu, Canva, Figma, and Microsoft SwiftKey, while giving creators, brands, and rights holders tools to distribute, manage, and control content.

content.title: KLIPY raises $3.8M from investors and joins Google AI Futures Fund to build the expression layer for GIFs, memes, stickers, clips, and AI media

headline: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to aid competition
summary:
STORY: Britain’s competition watchdog has ordered Google to give greater transparency on how its search rankings work.

It’s part of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses.

And addresses concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.

The regulator said Google must also rank organic search results using objective criteria and introduce clearer complaint processes. 

Google said it will work with the watchdog, adding that its ranking systems are fair and transparent.

These new measures follow UK watchdog requirements announced earlier this month, 

which enable publishers to stop their content being used to power Google's AI features.

Google in March said it was developing new search controls to address British competition concerns.

The regulator said Google has six months to implement the fair ranking requirement - and warns of further measures if necessary. 

This adds to the increasing regulatory scrutiny the tech giant is facing around the world, including in the U.S. and EU.

content.title: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to aid competition
content.summary:
STORY: Britain’s competition watchdog has ordered Google to give greater transparency on how its search rankings work.

It’s part of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses.

And addresses concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.

The regulator said Google must also rank organic search results using objective criteria and introduce clearer complaint processes. 

Google said it will work with the watchdog, adding that its ranking systems are fair and transparent.

These new measures follow UK watchdog requirements announced earlier this month, 

which enable publishers to stop their content being used to power Google's AI features.

Google in March said it was developing new search controls to address British competition concerns.

The regulator said Google has six months to implement the fair ranking requirement - and warns of further measures if necessary. 

This adds to the increasing regulatory scrutiny the tech giant is facing around the world, including in the U.S. and EU.

headline: AI startup Genspark valued at $2.6 billion in latest funding round
summary: Technology company Genspark.ai said on Wednesday it had raised $100 million in its extended funding round ‌that valued it at $2.6 billion, underscoring strong investor appetite ‌for AI companies developing workplace productivity tools. The round was backed by existing investors ​including Sozo Ventures, Korea Mirae Asset and UpHonest Capital. The latest investment brings the Palo Alto, California-based company's total Series B financing to $485 million.

The latest investment brings the Palo Alto, California-based company's total Series B financing to $485 million.

headline: Allbirds rebrands as Smartbird in AI pivot, hires former AWS executive as CEO; shares soar
summary: Allbirds changed its name to Smartbird on Wednesday and appointed former Amazon executive Nadia Carlsten as ‌CEO, cementing the former footwear maker's pivot to an AI ‌infrastructure firm and sending its shares up more than 30%.  The company had said in ​April it was shifting its focus to offer cloud computing capacity and AI services, leading to a more than five-fold surge in its shares.  • ‌Carlsten brings AI and ⁠quantum computing experience from DCAI, Alphabet spinoff SandboxAQ, and Amazon Web Services, and has advised the World Economic ⁠Forum on computing and AI.

headline: Dialpad Brings Real-Time Customer Intelligence to Google Workspace and Gemini Enterprise
summary: SAN RAMON, Calif., June 17, 2026--Dialpad, the AI platform for customer experience, today announced a new integration with Google’s Gemini Enterprise for Google Workspace. The integration transforms how enterprises access and act on customer intelligence: business conversations happening on Dialpad become a source of synthesized, actionable insight inside Gmail, Docs, and Chat.

headline: Big Tech-backed coalition for carbon removal increases funding by $915 million, adds Anthropic
summary: A coalition backed by Big Tech companies for ​carbon removal technology, said on Wednesday it would inject an additional $915 million into the sector, and added Anthropic as a new participant. Frontier, launched in ‌2022 by companies ⁠including Stripe and Google, aims to help scale carbon removal technologies by committing ⁠to buy credits in advance, thereby derisking the projects and helping them grow more quickly. The additional ​funding brings ​its total funding ​pledges to $1.8 billion.
